scope_summary.problem: requirements.md: after ~130 sequential generator thread::spawn calls on macOS aarch64, JIT code execution crashes with EXC_BAD_ACCESS code=257 (PAC failure). Thread counts stay at 2 — threads ARE joined — but cumulative pthread lifecycle corrupts process state.
scope_summary.success_criteria: Generator stress test (200+ iterations with list comprehensions) passes without SIGBUS/SIGSEGV. Conformance suite can enable >99 active tests. Existing generator tests (basic_yield, send_throw, yield_from) remain passing.

boundary.in_scope: generator.rs: replace thread::spawn in ensure_started() with lazy-initialized pool of 2-4 worker threads. Workers receive (body_fn, args, channels, shared_capture) and set up thread_local GEN_TX/GEN_RX per task.
boundary.out_of_scope: Generator state machine (R1-R6 in generator.md unchanged). Async runtime. JIT codegen. Conformance test runner changes.
boundary.constraints: Generator spec (generator.md) defines state machine semantics but does not mandate threads — thread pool is a compatible implementation change. Thread-safe runtime spec (thread-safe-runtime.md R5/R7) requires global registries use Arc<Mutex>/DashMap + AtomicU64 counters — pool must follow this pattern.
